what_it_does: Empirically measures whether query vectors produced with 8-bit weights are compatible with an existing bf16-embedded search corpus for perplexity-ai/pplx-embed-context-v1-4b. It pulls real summary texts, obtains bf16 reference vectors from a live :8007 service, re-embeds the same texts offline with 8-bit weights on CPU, and compares cosine similarity and cross-dtype top-k neighbor overlap in the bf16 corpus. It also reports weight footprint and writes a JSON result file.
observations: Uses a live :8007 embeddings endpoint as the bf16 reference path while running the 8-bit arm CPU-only so it cannot disturb the live GPU.
Holds output quantization fixed at int8 in both arms so the only variable is weight dtype.
Measures operational compatibility by top-k overlap: neighbors of each 8-bit query vector vs neighbors of each bf16 query vector, both searched in the same large bf16 pool.
Includes a pre-populated findings docstring dated 2026-06-28 with mean cosine ~0.996 and overlap@10 ~0.965.
Reports actual weight footprint in GB by summing parameter element sizes, showing ~35% savings for 8-bit.
Writes results to a hardcoded /tmp/claude-1000/... path.
Uses trust_remote_code=True and model.encode() with batch_size=4 and normalize_embeddings=False.
Assumes query items are the first N rows of the pool so self_idx is simply range(N).
standards: Safety/isolation design: 8-bit evaluation runs CPU-only and never touches the live GPU.
Uses cross-dtype top-k overlap in a real dense corpus as the operational verdict metric rather than just per-vector cosine.
Docstring doubles as a lab notebook with method, dates, environment, and findings.
conventions: Heavy docstring narrates purpose, method, findings, and run command.
Environment variables override N, model, max tokens, and pool size.
Nested helper functions defined inside topk_overlap and main.
Uses numpy float64 for cosine and top-k computations to reduce numerical error.
concerns: Hardcoded absolute paths for CORPUS and the output JSON make the script fragile outside the author's machine.
The output path /tmp/claude-1000/-home-tim/... looks like a scratchpad artifact and may not exist or be writable.
Assumes the live service is healthy and reachable at 127.0.0.1:8007 with no retry or error handling.
trust_remote_code=True increases execution risk for an unpinned custom model.
The assert that CUDA is unavailable will crash if the environment is not correctly restricted.
Self-index assumption only holds if pool_texts[:N] are exactly the query texts; any filtering or spread logic change would invalidate the overlap metric.
No random seed or stratified sampling; the first N/linearly spread rows may not be representative.
notes: A one-off measurement script that treats the live embedding service as the bf16 oracle and an offline CPU 8-bit path as the challenger, then decides whether flipping PPLX_EMBED_8BIT=1 requires a full re-embed.
questions: Why is the output path hardcoded under /tmp/claude-1000/-home-tim/...?
How is the live :8007 service guaranteed to be using bf16 weights and int8 output?
Does bnb's CPU 8-bit path truly match the GPU 8-bit path for this model?
Why set tokenizer.model_max_length inside a try/except rather than using the model's config?
What happens if the corpus file is missing or has fewer than pool_n entries?
Is top-k overlap with self-exclusion sufficient to capture real retrieval quality degradation?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file does exactly what its docstring describes—compares 8-bit and bf16 query embeddings against a live bf16 corpus and reports cosine and top-k overlap.
apparent_themes: Measure what matters: neighbor retrieval overlap, not just vector similarity
intention_signals: The author wants to generate evidence that switching the embedding server to 8-bit weights is safe for the existing bf16-embedded corpus, framing the result as a go/no-go verdict for PPLX_EMBED_8BIT=1.
novelty: It uses live production vectors as the reference distribution while running the challenger quantization path offline on CPU, and it evaluates compatibility via cross-dtype top-k neighbor overlap in a real dense search pool rather than simple pairwise cosine.
contribution: A self-contained measurement harness that compares 8-bit and bf16 query embeddings against a live bf16 search corpus, reporting per-vector cosine similarity, cross-dtype top-k overlap, and weight footprint, suitable for informing a quantization rollout decision.
summary_for_embedding: A Python measurement script that evaluates whether 8-bit-weight embeddings for pplx-embed-context-v1-4b are compatible with an existing bf16-embedded corpus by comparing live bf16 vectors against offline CPU 8-bit vectors using cosine similarity and top-k neighbor overlap.
intention_for_embedding: To provide empirical evidence that enabling 8-bit weights does not require re-embedding the existing bf16 search corpus.

symbols.does: Computes cosine similarity between two vectors in float64.
symbols.description: Normalizes both vectors and returns their dot product; returns 0.0 if either norm is zero.

symbols.does: Loads n real, non-trivial summary texts spread across the corpus.
symbols.description: Wraps _load_texts with spread=True to sample summaries that are at least 20 characters long and deduplicated.

symbols.does: Reads the extractions JSONL and selects texts.
symbols.description: When spread=True it picks roughly evenly spaced rows; otherwise it reads sequentially. Filters for non-empty, non-duplicate summaries or 'about' fields.

symbols.does: Fetches bf16-path embeddings from the live :8007 service with int8 output.
symbols.description: Sends one text at a time to the local v1/embeddings endpoint, requesting int8 quantization and no normalization, and stacks the returned vectors.

symbols.does: Sums the byte footprint of all model parameters and buffers.
symbols.description: Deduplicates tensors by id and multiplies element count by element size, intended to report real on-device storage including 1-byte Int8Params.

symbols.does: Loads the model offline on CPU and encodes texts with either 8-bit or bf16 weights.
symbols.description: Uses BitsAndBytesConfig(load_in_8bit=True) for the 8-bit arm or torch.bfloat16 for the control arm, then calls model.encode with int8 output and returns vectors plus weight bytes.

symbols.does: Computes mean top-k neighbor overlap between two query vector sets against a shared corpus.
symbols.description: For each query, it excludes the corpus self row, finds the top-k neighbors using cosine scores, and returns the mean Jaccard-style overlap between the two rankings.
Nested helper inside topk_overlap that replaces zero norms with 1.0 to avoid division by zero.

symbols.does: Orchestrates the full 8-bit vs bf16 measurement and prints/writes results.
symbols.description: Loads the pool and query texts, embeds the pool via the live service, embeds queries offline in both bf16 and 8-bit, computes cosines and top-k overlaps, prints statistics, and saves a JSON result file.

symbols.does: Formats mean, min, 10th percentile, and max of an array.
symbols.description: Nested helper inside main used to print cosine similarity statistics in a compact string.
